steps.title: Identify the defining contrast in the claim
steps.content: The claim specifies that the speaker criticizes activists who advocate for humanitarian causes in OTHER COUNTRIES while overlooking LOCAL CONCERNS. The key signal is this explicit contrast: championing distant causes versus ignoring nearby suffering.

steps.title: Find the quotation capturing both parts of the contrast
steps.content: Choice B directly expresses this two-part criticism: “When ye plead for the wrecked and fallen, / The exile from far-distant shores” shows activists championing distant causes, while “Remember that men are still wasting / Life's crimson around your own doors” highlights overlooked local suffering. The other three choices lack this dual structure—A addresses divine justice, C celebrates patriotic values, and D promotes spreading inspirational songs, none capturing the tension between advocacy for distant causes and neglect of local concerns. That matches choice B — B is correct.
